metrics.narrative: No quantified savings, cycle-time, or efficiency metric has surfaced in any call or Next Step across four months. Rouven Ehniss told the Zycus team on 6 Mar 2026 that WIK needs 'quick wins' to secure buy-in from the organization and board for its broader digitalization strategy, but no number has since been produced to support that ask, leaving the business case qualitative only.

champion.narrative: Rouven Ehniss (GM, Global Sourcing) has been the consistent driver since the 6 Mar 2026 discovery call, attending all five identified touchpoints (Mar-May 2026), coordinating the NDA, submitting use cases, and pushing the $150k proposal into WIK's internal budget process (6 Jun 2026). He remains engaged as of 2 Jul 2026 (paired with Hagan Brown on the next revert). However, he told Zycus on 6 Mar 2026 that the initiative needs 'buy-in of the organization,' meaning he lacks unilateral authority to approve the spend, which caps his champion strength at developing rather than fully empowered.

competition.narrative: No rival vendor has been named for this opportunity in any Salesforce field, call, or Next Step entry across four months. The live competitive dynamic is status quo / no-decision: the $150k proposal has sat in internal budget review since 6 Jun 2026 with no confirmed approval date, and the declined 22 Jun 2026 Horizon leadership review increases the risk the initiative simply stalls rather than being lost to a named competitor.

identify_pain.narrative: Kelvin Loh (Head of IT) described WIK's in-house office-automation (OA) platform as handling purchasing requisition forms and approval flows with limited integration options, on the 6 Mar 2026 call ('the current OA system primarily handles purchasing requisition forms and their associated approval flows, indicating a need for potential integrations'). The Zycus regional sales lead framed WIK's broader pain as unstructured, multi-channel procurement requests creating inefficiency, which Kelvin Loh's integration questions and the prepared use cases from Karen Zheng and a Global Indirect Sourcing colleague on the same call corroborated.

paper_process.narrative: Only an NDA has been executed so far (by 18 Mar 2026, per Next Step). No MSA, Order Form, or SOW discussion has surfaced in any call or Next Step, consistent with the deal still being in Formal Evaluation rather than a contracting stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has directly engaged with the relevant stakeholder Step notes (22 Jun and 2 Jul 2026 entries) reference a CPO who was expected to attend the 22 Jun 2026 Horizon event to review the ANA proposal with leadership; that person declined and has never joined a call, and is not recorded as a named Salesforce contact for this account. Rouven Ehniss, the highest-engaged contact (GM, Global Sourcing, tagged Evaluator on the contact role), told the Zycus team on 6 Mar 2026 that any initiative would need 'buy-in of the organization,' confirming he cannot approve the $150k spend himself.

decision_process.narrative: The process has run: discovery call (6 Mar 2026) -> NDA execution and use-case submission (by 18 Mar 2026) -> clarification/review meetings (14 Apr, 28 Apr, 14 May 2026) -> proposal issued and sent to WIK management for budget approval (6 Jun 2026) -> a planned leadership review at the Horizon event (22 Jun 2026), which the referenced CPO declined to attend. The process is currently stalled at the internal budget-approval step with no confirmed decision date; Rouven Ehniss and Hagan Brown said on 2 Jul 2026 they would revert shortly.

decision_criteria.narrative: WIK asked Zycus to assess two specific AI use cases (Merlin Intake and ANA) for feasibility, cost, and Hong Kong/China regulatory fit before any workshop-style POC, per the 6 Mar 2026 call. Those use cases were submitted and a formal proposal was issued (Next_Step__c, 6 Jun 2026 entry), showing the evaluation criteria have narrowed to specific agentic-AI use cases rather than a broad RFP against many vendors.

day_summary.overall: Carl chased WIK for a status update after weeks of silence following their decline of the Horizon Singapore event invite, and Rouven Ehniss finally responded confirming their internal review of the agentic AI proposal is still ongoing, with a next-steps outline promised by end of July. The deal remains stalled on WIK's side with no new commitment beyond a vague timeline — Zycus is now waiting on them, having done what it can to nudge the process along.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor has surfaced in any call, field, or Next_Step across the 4-month cycle (6 Mar - 2 Jul 2026). The only competitor references in the record are Zycus's own win stories against SAP Ariba, Coupa and GEP cited for OTHER customers during the 6 Mar 2026 intro pitch, not rivals WIK is evaluating. As of 2 Jul 2026 this reads as a sole-vendor evaluation; the real threat is not a competitor but inertia - an unapproved budget and a CPO who has never engaged.
